input.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

raw.headline: Ed Sheeran has already planned his posthumous album

winner: I knew Ed Sheeran was planning ahead—turns out he even picked his own graveyard spot for the posthumous album release party. "Thinking Out Loud" took on a whole new meaning when they found him sketching headstones with song lyrics and a list labeled 'Tombstone Hits.'

candidates.text: I knew Ed Sheeran was planning ahead—turns out he even picked his own graveyard spot for the posthumous album release party. "Thinking Out Loud" took on a whole new meaning when they found him sketching headstones with song lyrics and a list labeled 'Tombstone Hits.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and specific, with a clever twist on Ed Sheeran's song title and the concept of a posthumous album."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran’s posthumous album is just a Spotify playlist we’ll all pretend he curated himself.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on posthumous albums, concise and specific with a modern touch (Spotify playlist), and natural humor."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album was such a hit that his pre-natal album is getting re-released.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a clever play on 'pre-natal' and 'posthumous,' concise and specific."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran's posthumous album is called "Sheeran of Us." The tracklist includes "Thinking Out Loud" and... well, that's about all he could think of before passing away.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever play on Ed Sheeran's name. It also includes a humorous nod to his most famous song."}

candidates.text: Ed Sheeran’s posthumous album is called *"Divide and Conquer"*—it’s 12 tracks of him dividing up all the money from his estate.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, playing on the title of Ed Sheeran's album 'Divide' with a pun involving money division. It’s humorous and adheres to natural English without being offensive or generic."}
